review_metadata.scope: Claude design review artifacts and automation readiness

findings.title: Identifiers are not enforced across review artifacts
findings.description: Markdown reviews lack deterministic identifiers, creating ambiguity when automation parses the actions file.
recommended_action: Adopt stable IDs in markdown that mirror the JSON schema expectations.
Add guidance in the template and README to keep markdown and JSON artifacts aligned.
findings.follow_up_trigger: After the template PR merges so identifier guidance is in place before the next review cycle.

findings.title: Missing machine-readable action payloads
findings.description: No JSON actions file accompanies the markdown review, preventing downstream automation from emitting GitHub issues.
recommended_action: Require a claude-review.actions.json artifact alongside every markdown review.
Validate the actions file against the schema before publishing.
findings.follow_up_trigger: Before enabling automated issue emission from the actions JSON.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Publish Claude actions schema and example

findings.title: Follow-up triggers are not encoded
findings.description: Triggers and due dates are not captured alongside actions, so reviews can stall without scheduled checkpoints.
recommended_action: Include follow-up triggers and due dates in the actions JSON and mirror them in the review registry.
findings.follow_up_trigger: After the first automation pilot run emits issues and registry updates are wired.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Encode follow-up triggers next to actions

executive_summary: Dual artifacts (markdown + JSON) are required for deterministic automation of Claude reviews.
Identifiers are currently informal; schema-backed IDs enable cross-repo ingestion.
Follow-up triggers must live next to actions to avoid stalled reviews.
strengths: Design review standard already defines stable section ordering.
Review registry exists for linking artifacts and follow-up scheduling.

gaps.statement: Markdown reviews lack enforced identifier conventions, making cross-repo ingestion brittle.
No machine-readable action file exists to translate recommendations into GitHub issues.
Follow-up triggers are not encoded alongside actions, reducing automation fidelity.

risks.statement: Automation may create incomplete or duplicated issues because IDs are not deterministic.
risks.rationale: Gaps in identifier discipline and missing machine-readable actions make mapping brittle.

risks.statement: Missing follow-up triggers causes reviews to stall without scheduled checkpoints.
risks.rationale: Actions do not yet encode due dates or triggers in a consumable format.

recommendations.statement: Adopt the Claude review template with stable IDs that mirror machine-readable actions; keep mapping explicit in the review artifact.
recommendations.expected_outcome: Reviews carry deterministic identifiers that downstream automation can parse and link.

recommendations.statement: Require a JSON actions file per review aligned to the schema so automation can emit GitHub issues with labels and targets.
recommendations.expected_outcome: Every review emits automation-ready data for issue creation and tracking.

recommendations.statement: Capture follow-up triggers and due dates next to actions and surface them in the registry.
recommendations.expected_outcome: Reviews stay on schedule and trigger follow-up when conditions are met.

actions.title: Publish Claude review template and identifier guidance
actions.description: Add a Claude-specific markdown template with stable IDs and ensure documentation explains how to keep markdown and JSON artifacts aligned.
actions.expected_artifact: Updated documentation and template in design-reviews/
acceptance_criteria: Template includes ID guidance that mirrors the JSON schema expectations.
README documents the dual-artifact requirement and naming conventions.
Example review references both the markdown and JSON artifacts.

actions.title: Provide JSON schema and example actions file
actions.description: Publish a machine-readable schema and sample actions file that map recommendations to automation-ready actions and GitHub routing metadata.
actions.expected_artifact: claude-review.schema.json with a validating example actions file.
acceptance_criteria: Schema uses stable IDs and disallows additionalProperties by default.
Example actions JSON validates against the schema and references the markdown artifact.
Actions include target repos and label suggestions for GitHub issue creation.

actions.title: Encode follow-up triggers next to actions
actions.description: Record due dates and triggers in the actions file and mirror them in the review registry when automation is enabled.
actions.expected_artifact: Actions JSON with explicit follow-up triggers and due dates, mirrored into review registry entries.
acceptance_criteria: Actions include due_date fields when timelines are known.
Follow-up triggers are present in the actions JSON and consumable by automation.
Registry entries link the markdown and actions artifacts when the review is recorded.
follow_up_triggers: After first automated issue creation run
